items.title: Treasury Market’s June Rally Bails Out Quarter and First Half
items.summary: US Treasuries were set to end June with a modest gain thanks to a collapse in inflation expectations, ending the market’s performance drought over the first five months of the year.

items.title: 'Circumstances Don't Call for Rate Hikes Right Now' Says Win Thin
items.summary:
The yen’s descent to a four-decade low is the talk of currency markets today, and the next big trigger for the selloff may be whether Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh keeps up his hawkish tone at the Sintra policymaker forum.
Win Thin, Chief Economist at Bank of Nassau, discusses the Yen, as well as his outlook on rates under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. (Source: Bloomberg)

With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: After U.S.-Iran War, Oman Is Said to Propose Strait of Hormuz Fee Plan
items.summary: Under the proposal, Iran and Oman would collect payment for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, according to officials and diplomats with knowledge of the matter.

items.title: Why the last Iran nuclear deal still threatens the next one

During a meeting with the National Security Advisor to the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, Jonathan Powell, we discussed continued support for Ukraine. We are grateful to the United Kingdom for its decision regarding a new support package worth nearly £290 million. It matters that our partners recognize Ukraine's achievements on the front lines, as well as the results of our mid-range and deep strikes. We discussed the situation on the battlefield, in Ukraine's temporarily occupied territories, including Crimea, and inside Russia – fuel shortages and shifting public sentiment. Particular attention was given to preparations for the NATO Summit in Ankara and diplomatic efforts to achieve peace. We also discussed potential ways to reinvigorate the diplomatic process and possible formats for future negotiations.
items.summary:

items.title: US Supreme Court upholds state bans on transgender student athletes
items.summary:
The US Supreme Court cleared the way on Tuesday for ⁠states to impose restrictions on transgender student athletes, upholding laws in West Virginia and Idaho banning them from female sports teams.
It delivered a major victory to conservatives in one of the country’s most fiercely contested culture-war battles.

EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

items.title: Oil Supply Won't Come Back as Quickly as Expected, Babin Says
items.summary: Rebecca Babin, Senior Equity Trader at CIBC Private Wealth, discusses the current state of oil market dynamics. She says the market is overestimating the speed at which oil supply will return to pre-conflict levels. Babin expects oil prices to settle between $75 and $80 per barrel by the end of the year. She speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)
